prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', seasonality_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=3.43) and high seasonality capture variance (1.06), with NaiveMovingAverage dominating both trend and seasonality metrics while THETA leads overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (ARIMA, DWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (THETA, ETS), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination as the recommended method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "THETA"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "rf", "ARIMA", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 3.4262,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=3.43) and high seasonality capture variance (1.06), with NaiveMovingAverage dominating both trend and seasonality metrics while THETA leads overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (ARIMA, DWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (THETA, ETS), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination as the recommended method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon specialization patterns requiring selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.267 (≥0.25) justifies robust approaches like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. RMSE spread ratio=3.43 shows extreme model disagreement, favoring DBA over simple mean. n_windows=3 is very low, so top_k=3 provides stability while still capturing diverse signals. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality capture variance (1.06). Score preset rmse_focus aligns with validation showing THETA as best model (RMSE=4290) vs worst CWT_svr (RMSE=22960)."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all strategies use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants but current candidates already have better scores (dba_combination: 0.805, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35: 0.809, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3: 0.809, best_per_horizon_by_validation: 0.867). No redundancy - all different types. Diversity rules satisfied (≥1 of each type present).", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=3.43) and high seasonality capture variance (1.06), with NaiveMovingAverage dominating both trend and seasonality metrics while THETA leads overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (ARIMA, DWT_catboost) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (THETA, ETS), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination as the recommended method to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating disagreement risks.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon specialization patterns requiring selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.267 (≥0.25) justifies robust approaches like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. RMSE spread ratio=3.43 shows extreme model disagreement, favoring DBA over simple mean. n_windows=3 is very low, so top_k=3 provides stability while still capturing diverse signals. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality capture variance (1.06). Score preset rmse_focus aligns with validation showing THETA as best model (RMSE=4290) vs worst CWT_svr (RMSE=22960).

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all strategies use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants but current candidates already have better scores (dba_combination: 0.805, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35: 0.809, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3: 0.809, best_per_horizon_by_validation: 0.867). No redundancy - all different types. Diversity rules satisfied (≥1 of each type present).
